parsed_review.summary: CP00-874 (RP29 Commercial Readiness, Risk A, 10 units, RP29.P01.M05.S14-RP29.P01.M06.S03) is a descriptor-only IncidentRunbook export/test/review tail + relationship-map foundation pack and passes hardened read-only review. PACK POSITIONING is legitimate: closeout-pack-plan.json live cursor shows last-closed CP00-873 with next_subphase RP29.P01.M05.S14 (this pack's first unit), the CP00-874 plan entry has range/units/risk matching the manifest, next pack is CP00-875 (RP29.P01.M06.S04), and source_ledger_sha (13c7ed65...) matches the baseline; the manifest plan_binding_snapshot and included_units match the live plan exactly with no drift; deliverable distribution (impl 4/test 3/hermes 1/claude 1/ui 1) and micro_title distribution (Permission And Audit Binding 7 / Synthetic Fixture Set 3) match. IMPLEMENTATION (packages/commercial/src/{registry,validators,model,states,index}.js) is pure frozen constant declarations plus validation functions; a grep confirmed NO writeFile/appendFile/child_process/spawn/fetch/http/process.env in the package or scripts/validate-rp29-commercial-readiness-contract.mjs; createSectionRows hardcodes every row's runtime/creation/write/receipt flags false, the CustomerPlan-not-implemented invariant holds (5 implemented models, CustomerPlan contract-shell only), and the IncidentRunbook->AuditEvent relationship is validator-enforced. TESTS (test/model.test.js) cover CP00-874 coverage-vs-live-plan, descriptor runtime-closure (incident_runbook_activated/customer_plan_mutated/audit_event_written all false), model-registry tenant-scoping + AuditEvent reference, invalid-reference and ownership-drift negatives, contract-projection currency, and the compatibility alias, wired into npm test and rp29:commercial:validate. CONTRACTS: commercial-readiness-contract.json latest_projection.descriptor=CommercialCp874IncidentRunbookRelationshipTailDescriptor, current/latest_pack=CP00-874, validation.valid=true with latest_pack_id/plan_pack_id=CP00-874, and top-level mandatory_artifacts/required_capabilities/safety_gates/no_leak_guards match CP874 requirements; the commercial-contract.json compatibility alias points to the canonical contract with latest_pack_id CP00-874. SAFETY/DATA: COMMERCIAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and all 10 case rows set descriptor_only=true with all runtime/write/real-data/credential flags false (validators reject any true); a direct secret/PII scan of the ~4,800-line contract and the package surfaced only false-valued attestation fields and guard names (no keys, tokens, private keys, emails, SSNs, or real client data). AUTHORITY BOUNDARY is correct: descriptor and contract assert claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-875); Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false and Claude packet read_only=true/promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, so Hermes/Claude patterns are deterministic review packet/receipt evidence only, not product runtime. PRE-PROMOTION STATES are correct and expected: manifest status in_progress with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false, command-evidence pending_review, construction-inspection and adjudication PENDING; validate-closeout-pack.mjs only enforces production_ready truth gates once status=production_ready, so nothing prematurely claims production, runtime, or enterprise trust. REVIEW-EVIDENCE HYGIENE (CP178+): prior attempt-01 (claude_cli API Error 529 Overloaded, 0 output tokens) was correctly normalized to invalid_review (valid_review=false, closeout_eligible=false, invalid_reason auth_or_cli_error; structured_output_missing; required_field_error: parsed_review missing, parsed_review=null); readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid-JSON result); claude-review-result valid_review_receipts=[] and validation-report confirms read-only tools (Read,Grep,Glob) with no forbidden tools and no omitted untracked implementation files, so no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-shaped output is counted as valid evidence. PROVENANCE: file-level consistency is directly verified through read-only tools in this review; the validation command exit codes recorded in command-evidence.json (commercial syntax check, rp29:commercial:validate, node --test — all exit 0) are agent-reported because Bash is unavailable to the reviewer, and the full deterministic suite (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements, weighted, fullplan, goal:closeout, rp00 control-plane, git diff --check) runs in the post-review deterministic step per standard closeout flow. SCOPE HYGIENE: changed-file-scope correctly lists the in-scope modified files (contracts x2, README, registry.js, validators.js, test, validate script) and classifies the progress-control-room UI files and workbook/docs untracked items as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (non-CP UI workstream). Minor non-blocking observation: M06 rows (Package directory layout / Primary entity identifier / Tenant scope field) carry deliverable_type ui and micro_title Synthetic Fixture Set from the upstream weighted-implementation-ledger generation; coverage validation is internally consistent (ui:1, Synthetic Fixture Set:3 expected and satisfied) and this upstream taxonomy label has no runtime or safety impact. No P0-P3 findings; nothing in the static evidence blocks pack or this CP closeout, and blocks_goal_closeout is false because this descriptor pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim.
